description: MCP profile policy for the Personal Projects workspace. Each profile defines allowed, required, disabled, and task-based MCP servers. Replaces the flat MCP policy from C1.

baseline.description: Minimal safe default for normal development repos. Small tool surface, no browser/design/research-heavy tools.

context7.rationale: Documentation lookup is universally useful and safe.

exa.rationale: Web search for grounded research without heavy browsing.

web-tools.rationale: DEPRECATED (2026-07-01): No service code, no MCP endpoint, no unique capability. Removed from active config. Capabilities covered by exa, webfetch, context7, github, playwright.

playwright.rationale: Browser automation not needed for baseline development. UI repos may enable through explicit profile/override.

pencil.rationale: Design tool not needed for baseline development. Enable only through explicit design workflow approval.

firecrawl.rationale: Heavy web crawling not needed for baseline development.

ui_ux.description: For repos where visual/browser validation matters: protected-repo, demo-project, example-dashboard. Supports browser evidence, visual QA, interaction testing, and design iteration.

web-tools.rationale: DEPRECATED (2026-07-01): No service code, no MCP endpoint, no unique capability. Removed from active config. Capabilities covered by exa, webfetch, context7, github, playwright.

playwright.rationale: Browser automation for UI verification, visual QA, screenshot evidence, interaction testing, and layout validation. Critical for UI/UX workflow.

pencil.rationale: Design tool for UI/UX design iteration. Enable if stable and useful in the OpenCode setup.

firecrawl.rationale: Heavy web crawling not needed for UI/UX work.

research.description: For product research, market analysis, compliance scanning, competitor review, and institutional product work. Controlled browsing without enabling heavy tools everywhere.

web-tools.rationale: DEPRECATED (2026-07-01): No service code, no MCP endpoint, no unique capability. Removed from active config. Capabilities covered by exa, webfetch, context7, github, playwright.

playwright.rationale: Browser automation for research verification when needed.

pencil.rationale: Design tool not needed for research.

firecrawl.rationale: Enable only when explicitly requested for deep web crawling research.

automation.description: For automation-heavy repos. Restricted MCP surface, no design/browser-heavy tools unless explicitly required.

web-tools.rationale: DEPRECATED (2026-07-01): No service code, no MCP endpoint, no unique capability. Removed from active config. Capabilities covered by exa, webfetch, context7, github, playwright.

playwright.rationale: Browser automation not needed unless explicitly required for E2E testing.

pencil.rationale: Design tool not needed for automation.

firecrawl.rationale: Heavy web crawling not needed for automation.

apa_product_factory.description: Governed special profile for apa-digital-asset-product. APA has domain-specific agents (blockchain/stablecoin, compliance/AML, treasury ops) and custom factory commands. Approved by ADR.

web-tools.rationale: DEPRECATED (2026-07-01): No service code, no MCP endpoint, no unique capability. Removed from active config. Capabilities covered by exa, webfetch, context7, github, playwright.

playwright.rationale: Enable only when UI deliverables or browser verification is needed.

pencil.rationale: Design tool not needed unless UI deliverables exist.

firecrawl.rationale: Enable only for research tasks (market analysis, competitor review, compliance scanning).

archived.description: For archived/excluded repos. All MCPs disabled. No active development expected.

enforcement.audit_mode: WARN on profile mismatches, document drift
enforcement.enforce_mode: FAIL on profile violations, block config changes
enforcement.switch_condition: After C3 migration complete and all guards pass in audit mode with 0 FAILs and 0 WARNs
